title: Compliance Receipt
description: Signed, content-addressed, RFC 8785-canonicalized record of a Scope Contract evaluation. Issued for every evaluation (allow, deny, or escalate). Offline-verifiable via embedded algorithm + key_id; algorithm-agile; multi-signature ready; hash-chained per agent. License: MIT (SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT). Part of AgentMarshal — github.com/ApexForge13/agentmarshal.

receipt_version.description: Compliance Receipt artifact version. Independent of schema_version so a v0.1 receipt format MAY be re-validated against a v0.2 schema if backward-compatible.

schema_version.description: Version of this JSON Schema document the receipt conforms to. Verifiers MAY select a schema by this field when running against a multi-version verifier.

receipt_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this receipt. RECOMMENDED: UUIDv4. Distinct from evaluation_id (which is the audit row identifier and MAY be shared by multiple receipts in rare re-issue scenarios).

receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized receipt body MINUS this field itself. Computed AFTER signatures are appended, over the union of the body and the signatures array. Provides content-addressing and an integrity check independent of signatures.

previous_receipt_hash.description: receipt_hash of the prior receipt in the agent's hash chain, or null when this is the first receipt for the agent (or chain history is not provided). Enables tamper-evident sequencing of an agent's actions over time.

canonical_form.description: Algorithm used to produce the canonical byte sequence that is signed and hashed. Declared in-band so future canonicalization rotations do not invalidate old receipts.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the receipt was constructed (after evaluation; before signing). Verifiers MAY use this with regulatory_state.anchor_timestamp to detect replay.

code_version.description: Identifier of the AgentMarshal codebase version that produced this receipt. RECOMMENDED: 40-char git SHA-1. Falls back to AGENTMARSHAL_CODE_VERSION env var or the literal 'unknown' when git is unavailable.

contract_id.description: contract_id of the Scope Contract this evaluation was performed against.

contract_version.description: Version label of the contract that was active at evaluation time. String to allow semver, supersession sequences, or operator-defined version labels.

tenant_id.description: Operator tenant namespace, echoed from the contract.

agent_id.description: Agent identity from the contract being evaluated against.

evaluation_id.description: Identifier of the underlying evaluation audit row. Joins the receipt to the full AuthZEN request/response that produced it.

request_id.description: AuthZEN X-Request-ID correlation identifier from the PEP call that triggered evaluation. Joins receipts to PEP-side traces.

decision.description: Scope Contract-level decision summary. The reason and reason_code are surfaced verbatim in operator review UIs.

effect.description: Decision applied to the request. 'allow' permits the action; 'deny' blocks it; 'escalate' defers to a human or higher-authority workflow.

evaluation_path.description: Phase of evaluation that produced the decision.

matched_rule_id.description: rule_id of the declared_scope rule that matched. Non-null only when evaluation_path is 'declared_scope'.

review_required.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 16 three-state). True when the decision blocks the action pending human review (some composite returned 'review' and none returned 'fail') rather than denying outright. Absent ⇒ false; backward-compatible with all pre-Bubble-16 receipts, which omit it. The decision.effect remains the strict allow/deny/escalate vocabulary; this field carries the richer status.

review_reason.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 16). Human-readable reason for the review hold; present only when review_required is true.

predicate_evaluations.description: Ordered trace of per-rule predicate evaluations performed during this evaluation. Useful for debugging false-negative matches and for compliance review.

composite_evaluations.description: Ordered trace of composite predicate evaluations (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, etc.). Composite results other than 'pass' block 'allow' decisions via the fail-safe policy.

bd_calls.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 17). Ordered audit entries for governed Bright Data tool calls made during this evaluation via the AgentMarshal MCP proxy. Absent ⇒ no BD calls (backward-compatible; pre-Bubble-17 receipts omit it). Part of the signed body, so tampering with any bd_call breaks the signature — no separate verification step required.

regulatory_state.description: Anchor that ties this receipt to a point-in-time snapshot of external regulatory data (e.g., DNC registry, state quiet-hours tables). When 'pending' is true, no anchor was available; Bright Data integration day populates real anchors.

hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized regulatory snapshot. Null when pending.

pending.description: True when no regulatory snapshot was available at evaluation time. Verifiers MAY downgrade trust accordingly.

snapshot_source.description: Identifier of the data source the snapshot was derived from. Null when pending.

anchor_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the snapshot was anchored to an external timestamping service. Null when pending or when no external anchor was used.

anchor_method.description: External anchoring method used. 'pending' is the v0.1 default until Bright Data integration day wires real anchors.

signatures.description: One or more signatures over the canonicalized body (the receipt MINUS receipt_hash, signatures, and timestamp_token). All listed signatures MUST verify for a receipt to be considered valid.

timestamp_token.description: OPTIONAL RFC 3161 external timestamp anchor over receipt_hash, proving WHEN the receipt was issued independent of AgentMarshal's clock. Attached AFTER signing and is NOT part of the signed body or receipt_hash (verifiers strip it before recomputing signed bytes, same as receipt_hash and signatures). null or absent ⇒ not externally timestamped (TSA unreachable at issuance, or a pre-timestamping receipt); such receipts remain signature-valid.

PredicateEvaluation.description: Per-predicate evaluation record echoed from the AuthZEN evaluator.

rule_id.description: rule_id within which this predicate was evaluated. Null when evaluation_path is 'out_of_scope' or 'temporal'.

predicate_path.description: Dot-path to the predicate within the match block.

constraint.description: Verbatim copy of the predicate value from the contract. May be a literal or an operator object.

actual_value.description: The value from the request at predicate_path. May be null when the property is absent.

result.description: Whether this predicate passed, failed, or was skipped.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for fail/skipped results.

CompositePredicateEvaluation.description: Per-composite predicate evaluation entry (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, etc.).

result.description: Composite result. 'pass' is allowable; 'fail', 'stub', and 'review' block allow under the fail-safe policy. 'review' (Bubble 16) marks a possible-match that blocks pending human review, distinct from a hard 'fail'.

reason.description: Human-readable reason produced by the composite predicate.

details.description: Open trace bag from the composite predicate. Schema permits arbitrary keys to keep predicate authors unconstrained.

BDCallAudit.description: Audit record of one governed Bright Data call captured into the receipt. Execution fields (executed_at, duration_ms, response_sha256, response_size_bytes, bd_request_id) are null when the call was denied (never executed) or when execution failed before a response was captured.

service.description: Bright Data service the call targeted.

matched_rule_id.description: rule_id of the bd_permissions rule that governed the call; null when no rule matched (deny).

governance_result.description: MCP-proxy governance decision for the call.

composite_outcomes.description: Outcomes of the bd_permissions rule's composite checks, in order.

executed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC time the BD call was executed; null when denied/not executed.

duration_ms.description: BD call wall-clock duration in ms; null when not executed.

response_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex of the raw BD response body (fingerprint); null when no response captured.

response_size_bytes.description: Byte length of the raw BD response; null when no response captured.

bd_request_id.description: Bright Data's own request id for cross-referencing; null when unavailable.

BDCompositeOutcome.description: Outcome of one composite check run during BD-call governance.

result.description: Composite result. 'pass' is allowable; 'fail', 'stub', and 'review' block the rule.

ReceiptSignature.description: One signature over the canonical body. signer_role indicates the party; multi-sig means all signatures MUST verify.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. v0.1 supports Ed25519 only; future versions MAY add additional algorithms via enum extension.

key_id.description: RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of the public key used, prefixed with 'am-'. Verifiers resolve this to a public key via the issuer's JWKS endpoint or a cached JWK Set.

public_key_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 hex of the raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key. Provided for human-readable identification and grep-ability; key_id is the canonical lookup identifier.

signature.description: Hex-encoded signature bytes. Length depends on algorithm (Ed25519 = 128 hex chars).

signed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this signature was produced.

signer_role.description: Role of the signing party. 'agentmarshal' is the substrate; 'operator' is the agent's principal; 'vendor' is a downstream system that received the authorized action.

TimestampToken.description: An RFC 3161 external timestamp token anchoring receipt_hash to a third-party Time Stamping Authority. v0.2 uses FreeTSA. The authoritative time and the hash binding are re-derived from token_b64 on verify; tsa and issued_at are informational mirrors.

token_b64.description: base64 of the DER-encoded RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (a CMS SignedData ContentInfo). Self-contained: embeds the TSA signing certificate so the token verifies offline against a pinned TSA root.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC genTime extracted from the token's TSTInfo at issuance. Informational; verifiers re-derive the authoritative time from token_b64.
